task: Write a Python function range_summary(nums) taking a list of integers, possibly unsorted with duplicates, and returning a string of the distinct values in ascending order, collapsing consecutive runs into 'start-end' and joining pieces with commas, so [1, 2, 3, 5] becomes '1-3,5'. A single number stays a single number, never a range. An empty list returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function meets all criteria including handling an empty list as an edge case.
trace:
**
I chose to solve this problem by first sorting and deduping the input list, then iterating over it to build up a list of ranges. I used a single flag `start` to keep track of whether we're in a range or not, and added logic to handle cases where a single number stays a single number (never a range). I also explicitly handled the edge case of an empty input list by returning an empty string immediately.
